Name: Cloudfoot Keep
Content: The party's home base and bastion located in the wilderness. Fortified keep where characters have personal quarters and can develop their resources. Contains Arc's mushroom garden, Vyphira's barracks with cloud-energy defenders, Fauda's stocked storehouse, Mask's spy network, and Sili's animal nests and outposts. Serves as a safe haven between adventures, currently well-maintained by servants. Controlled by the party. Key characters include all party members and keep servants. During visits, characters received updates on their bastion developments and planned their next moves. In Session 45, the keep was set in motion toward The Thundermaw Range, traveling over muddy grasslands with the party and their new allies aboard. Session 46, the Keep was ambushed by Solaran bounty hunters, leading to most of the party being captured and transported into a magical bottle realm. The Keep continues its journey southwest. In Session 71, the party used Transport Via Plants to return here for downtime, training, and crafting before heading toward Gloomwatch Sanctum.

Name: Travel Route to Tempest's Refuge
Content: Overland route through plains northwest of Greenmurk Forest, about two days travel on foot. Features open fields, foothills with caves, and known traveler shelters. Significant as the path where party experienced Imminent Convergence storm and cannibal cave encounter. Points of interest include storm-shelter caves. Key characters encountered include cannibals and storm refugees. During travel, party survived massive storm through Epic Skill Challenge, took shelter in cannibal cave, fought cannibals, and continued toward Tempest's Refuge.

Name: Tempest's Refuge
Content: Mountain settlement carved into mountainsides bearing large glowing storm runes. More subdued than Stormhaven, serves as refuge from storms. Contains stone gates, market districts, residential areas, and tunnel systems. Significant as location where Mask's healing attempt turned into murder investigation. Controlled by city guards and mages. Key characters include Elandra Hallowheart, Flora Mourne, Drayce Goldmere, Oswin Fellcrest, and Gale operative. During visits, party sought healing for Mask, murdered Elandra, fled authorities, confronted Gale operative, and helped changeling refugees escape. Later, the party returned after defeating Xyrx, reuniting Vy with her family and meeting Callie Black and Eredan Windsight.

Name: Shieldweaver's Armorer & Forge
Content: Armorer shop in Tempest's Refuge, only known armorer in the city. Contains display counter, workshop area, and loom. Significant as location where Vyphira discovered her stolen armor for sale. Controlled by Sorrax Shieldweaver. Key characters include Sorrax Shieldweaver, Garman, and discussed Voraxik Valarnn. During visit, Vyphira confronted Garman about her stolen armor, learned about tool theft from Sorrax, and promised to recover stolen tools.

Name: Ironhowl Forge
Content: Blacksmith forge in Drifthold located in The Forestride leg of the city. Contains custom-made safe under burning forge for valuable tools. Significant as target for Voraxik's attempted theft and location of third known set of special tools. Controlled by Torva Ironhowl. Key characters include Torva Ironhowl. During planned visit, party set trap for thieves during Torva's dinner break, leading to an ambush, a fight in the sewers below, and the recovery of the tools.

Name: Cannibal Cave
Content: Cave along travel route, well-known shelter used by travelers. Contains fire pit, roasting spit, and hidden human bone chime outside. Significant as location of brutal cannibal encounter. Temporarily controlled by cannibals posing as travelers. Key characters include five cannibal men. During visit, party accepted false hospitality, discovered cannibalism through half-dismembered corpse, fought brutal battle where Lady Seriss was nearly killed, and barricaded entrance for night.

Name: Greenmurk Forest
Content: Forest area party traveled through before reaching plains. Starting point of journey to Tempest's Refuge. Contains dense woodland paths. Significant as departure point from civilization. No key characters specifically mentioned. During travel, party left forest and entered storm-prone plains.

Name: Drifthold
Content: Massive six-legged stone city built on high stone shelf with trailing mobile extension called The Tailstride. Divided into tiers: base level, Stormforged Ring, Sky Ward. Contains universities, temples, markets, and residential districts. Significant as current major hub for refugees and location of major plot developments. Controlled by king and leg mages including Vale twins. Key characters include Gloriosa, Torva Ironhowl, Lord Crimm, Jorren and Megara Vale, Lyanna Vale, and Lucien Shinwell. During visits, party entered through brutal combat requirement, met Gloriosa, pursued various jobs, studied runes, investigated dragon cult, fulfilled a blood pact, discovered a secret rebellion in the sewers, and is now facing an imminent giant invasion. In Sessions 44-45, the city became the battleground against Storm Lord forces and the party's departure point for their journey to the Thundermaw Range.

Name: Tranquil Hollow Healing House & Hospital
Content: Healing facility in quieter market district of Tempest's Refuge. Contains stone pedestal for diagnostic rituals, healing supplies, and records. Significant as location where Elandra Hallowheart diagnosed Mask and was subsequently murdered. Controlled by Elandra Hallowheart. Key characters include Elandra Hallowheart and Flora Mourne. During visit, Elandra used flame ritual to diagnose Mask's fractured mind, then was murdered by party, leading to city-wide manhunt.

Name: Multi-faith Church (Tempest's Refuge)
Content: Church in Tempest's Refuge used by multiple faiths. Contains altar with liftable floor tile leading to basement safe-room. Significant as changeling refugee hiding place and location of confrontation with Gale forces. Controlled by Flora Mourne and refugees. Key characters include Flora Mourne, Veil, Shagra Wordbinder, Drath Wordbinder, and changeling refugees. During visits, party found refugee safe-room, confronted Flora about Elandra's murder, helped refugees escape, and fought Gale goblins.

Name: Solara
Content: Floating city above Eryndor, also called The Land of a Thousand Kingdoms. Contains myriad Bottle Kingdoms—entire worlds existing side by side. Ruled by ancient Greatwyrms and powerful noble houses. Significant as origin of changelings, headquarters of The Gale, and home to bounty hunter Calyx Newdusk. Controlled by Solaran rulers. Key characters include Broin, House Duskvale, House Honey, Lord Bainridge, and ancient dragon Voryxia. In Session 46, the party learned that House Duskvale hired Calyx to capture them in retaliation for destroying a House Honey gathering facility. Later, the party discovered House Octo's involvement with Xyrx and that Lady Seriss is being held there for marriage. Current status: The party's ultimate destination.

Name: The Ever-Burning Temple
Content: Great temple to Yondar in Drifthold, tallest in temple district. Contains carved stone entrance depicting Yondar's history, massive braziers, column of fire rising to glowing sphere tower. Significant as location where party communicated with Elandra through flames and learned about Yondari faith. Controlled by Yondari clergy. Key characters include Radiant priest Lucan Brightmere, Flamebearer paladins, and Yeldur worshippers outside. During visits, party learned about Yondar-Yeldur prophecy, communicated with resurrected Elandra, received offer of redemption trials, and had the corrupted Zrak'Torr purified.

Name: Crimm Manor
Content: Massive stone manor in Drifthold's Sky Ward with crest featuring large 'C'. Contains grand study with bookshelves, paintings, sculptures, ornate chair on dais, enormous organ, fine desk, and leather workshops. Significant as home of copper dragon lord and location where party received draconic boons. Controlled by Lord Halen Crimm. Key characters include Lord Crimm, Alira Crimm, Teren Vallin, Tolliver, and various artists. During visits, party accepted rescue mission, returned with rescued Alira, received draconic boons and information about Solara, and in Session 43, used it as a base to gather information about 'Rusty' Lou and learned of the giant emissary's threat.

Name: The Hollow Basilica
Content: Ancient chapel dedicated to earth dragon Khaimyrax beneath The Ashen Ledger warehouse. Contains enormous calcified dragon eggs, massive dragon skeleton, pool of blood, tall pedestal with staircase. Significant as dragon cult ritual site and location of major battle. Controlled by The Ashen Coil cult. Key characters include Seroth Kael, Brenna Kael, cultists, and Ghost Dragon. During visit, cult attempted blood ritual using Alira, party intervened, fought cultists and Ghost Dragon, rescued Alira.

Name: The Silver Vein Tavern
Content: Workman's bar in Drifthold's base level. Contains bar, tables, kitchen, brewery. Significant as location where party found Derron Thane and fought dragon cult attackers. Controlled by barkeep who takes 10% of bard earnings. Key characters include Derron Thane and dragon cult attackers. During visit, party located Derron, learned about his debt and Alira's kidnapping, fought cult members including transformed kobolds.

Name: The Ashen Ledger
Content: Antiquities import and export business in Drifthold's Stormforged Ring. Contains counter, ledger, shelves with artifacts, warehouse with magical crates, hidden trapdoor to basilica. Significant as front for dragon cult operations. Controlled by Seroth and Brenna Kael. Key characters include Seroth Kael, Brenna Kael, kobold guards, and cat-dragon creature. During visit, party confronted rude Seroth, learned from Brenna about Derron's discovery, later raided warehouse and discovered hidden basilica.

Name: Mossgate Hollow
Content: Luxury apartment building in Drifthold's Titan's Crown district. Contains hand-drawn elevator operated by ogre, penthouse suite with lavish sitting room. Significant as home of romance novelist Liora Quillheart. Controlled by Liora Quillheart. Key characters include Liora Quillheart and refined butler. During visit, party learned about Tana-Yondar history, The Lovers cult, and received directions to Ever-Burning Temple. In Session 42, Mask attended a gathering here as Dahlia.

Name: The Gull and Gasket
Content: Tavern and restaurant in Drifthold with extensive menu featuring storm-themed food and drinks. Contains job board with various postings. Significant as location where party reunited with Gloriosa and selected retrieval job. Controlled by tavern owners. Key characters include Gloriosa and her acolytes. During visit, party ate storm-themed meal, discussed childhood with Gloriosa, examined job board, and selected Crimm retrieval job.

Name: The Silver Hearth Inn
Content: High-end well-guarded inn in Drifthold. Contains suites for group accommodation, minibar, option for personal servants. Significant as party's temporary residence in Drifthold. Controlled by inn management. No key characters specifically mentioned. During stay, party rested, Vyphira acquired snacks, Mask taught Arc sign language, and they prepared for retrieval job.

Name: Drifthold University
Content: Educational institution in Drifthold where Drath Wordbinder teaches. Contains classrooms, research facilities for runic theory. Significant as location where party studied storm rune theory. Controlled by university administration. Key characters include Drath Wordbinder. During visit, party spent several hours studying runic theory under Drath's guidance, improving their understanding of magical runes.

Name: The Tether's End Tavern
Content: Tavern located at far end of Drifthold, rowdy and crowded. Contains main bar area, back rooms leading to alleys. Significant as initial meeting place where party expected to find Gloriosa. Controlled by tavern owners. Key characters include sneering man and pink-haired elf who observed party. During visit, party found Gloriosa wasn't present, were observed suspiciously, then led to magistrate's office.

Name: Magistrate's Office & Courtroom
Content: Unlit magistrate's office in Drifthold leading into courtroom through winding alleys and stairs. Contains judge's chair, courtroom seating. Significant as location where party reunited with Gloriosa and began Mask's mind healing. Controlled by Gloriosa and her followers. Key characters include Gloriosa and her acolytes. During visit, party had emotional reunion with Gloriosa, discussed Mask's condition, and prepared for mindscape journey.

Name: Stormhaven
Content: City mentioned but not visited in current sessions. Larger and less subdued than Tempest's Refuge. Significant as location where party previously dealt with Johnny Whispers and had other adventures referenced by Gale operative. Controlled by city authorities. Key characters include Johnny Whispers. Not visited in sessions 26-43, but referenced as previous location.

Name: Thornheath
Content: Northern city, birthplace and capital of Yondar's faith. Contains main Yondari temple complex. Significant as home of Hallowheart family and center of Yondari religion. Controlled by Yondari church and Lord Preston Hallowheart. Key characters include Velma Hallowheart and Preston Hallowheart. Not visited by party, but referenced as important religious center.

Name: The Tailstride
Content: Trailing mobile extension following behind main Drifthold city. Contains residential areas for refugees and less wealthy residents. Significant as safe location where Drath and Shagra brought changeling refugees. Controlled by city authorities. Key characters include Drath Wordbinder, Shagra Wordbinder, and Veil. During escape, refugees were brought here for safety away from main city scrutiny.

Name: The Echoing Spire
Content: Location in Drifthold controlled by Jorren and Megara Vale. One of the six walking legs of the city. Contains Vale residence and operations center. Significant as headquarters of Vale mages who held the party's blood pact. Controlled by Vale twins. Key characters include Jorren and Megara Vale, Lyanna Vale. During visit in Session 42, party met Lyanna and then the Vales, received new tasks after blood pact completion. In Session 43, the party discovered and used a secret passage from here into the sewers to meet Lucien Shinwell.

Name: The Clockspindle
Content: Shop in Drifthold displaying inverted King's Crown symbol of the Underking. Contains black market connections and stolen goods. Significant as location where Vyphira attempted to acquire stolen tools through Thieves' Cant. Controlled by shopkeeper with underworld connections. Key characters include anonymous shopkeeper. During visit, Vyphira indirectly requested acquisition of stolen tools, instructed to return in two days for news.

Name: Mask's Mindscape
Content: Metaphysical location inside Mask's subconscious during healing ritual. Contains semi-ethereal hallway with doors to memories, orphanage, Gale training facility, Stormsurge town, and crumbling Mind Palace. Significant as battlefield against parasitic persona Fendril. Controlled by Mask's consciousness and invading Fendril entity. Key characters include Fendril, Jin, Urdesh Darkmoore, Miss Amber, and Solenne. During visit, party battled Fendril's manifestations, rescued positive memory entities, and restored Mask's mental stability.

Name: Yondar's Flame Realm
Content: Endless realm of fire within the Ever-Burning Temple brazier. Contains divine fire landscape where Elandra communicates. Significant as secure communication channel with resurrected Elandra. Controlled by Yondar's divine power. Key characters include Elandra Hallowheart in transformed radiant state. During visit, party learned about redemption trials, Yondar-Yeldur prophecy, and received guidance about Solara and Tana.

Name: Travel Route to Drifthold
Content: Road east from Tempest's Refuge toward Drifthold. Features crowded refugee traffic, families with loaded carts, and Drath's enchanted tent campsite. Significant as escape route for changeling refugees and journey to current hub. Points of interest include Drath's rune-inscribed storm protection tent. Key characters include Drath Wordbinder, Shagra Wordbinder, Veil, and changeling refugees. During travel, party protected refugees, Mask shared true backstory, and group studied runic theory.

Name: Caeloria
Content: The kingdom ruled by House Stormrider. Known for its rapid growth, which attracted the attention of The Gale. Mask's original assignment from The Gale was to infiltrate this kingdom and discover the secret behind its growth. Lady Seriss is a noble from this kingdom. The party was traveling here when Lady Seriss joined them. The great city and capital of The Blackcloud Reach, protected by the Stormheart Amulet. The party was hired to recover the stolen amulet and find Lady Seriss. Current status: Concern is growing that Lady Seriss will marry in Solara, and the Blackcloud Coven cannot contact her.

Name: The Haven
Content: A settlement or neighborhood where Fauda lived for a time after leaving her sister Gloriosa. It was here that she befriended the orcish woman Olga Patter, who became a surrogate sister to her. Represents a period of relative peace in Fauda's life before the current adventures.

Name: Tallyport & Family Farm
Content: A coastal settlement near where Fauda and Gloriosa grew up on their parents' farm. They lived an ordinary life working as horse grooms. This was the site of their quiet and peaceful childhood before their paths diverged.

Name: Tempest's Watch
Content: The Dragonborn enclave where Vyphira, Voraxik Valarnn, Kharzid Tazek, and Shavara Sablefang all grew up together. It's where Vy first learned her craftsmanship and where her parents owned the special tools that were later stolen. The troublemakers Voraxik, Kharzid, and Shavara boasted of powerful associates from outside the enclave. A Storm Outpost and Dragonborn enclave.

Name: The Gale Training Facility
Content: A secret facility where The Gale trains its changeling agents. Mask was taken here after being purchased from Miss Amber's orphanage at age five. Here, they met Jin and his family, trained under Urdesh Darkmoore, and were indoctrinated into The Gale's operations. Both Mask and Jin received their Gale tattoos here upon graduation.

Name: Miss Amber's Orphanage
Content: The orphanage where Mask was left as a baby and lived until age five. Run by a woman named Miss Amber, who called Mask by their original name, Fendril. It was from here that The Gale purchased Mask, beginning their life as an agent.

Name: Stormsurge
Content: A town that appears in Mask's memories and mindscape. It features a central road with shops and a gallows. A woman named Solenne, who was important to Mask, lived here. The town represents a place where Mask feared the consequences of being an openly known changeling.

Name: The Lightning Strike Site
Content: A location along a travel route where Mask was struck by lightning while traveling with a Storm Guide. Instead of dying, Mask gained their magical abilities and had a vision of Solara - a colossal city hidden in storms with a voice whispering about 'the heart of the storm.' This event was a turning point that led Mask to question their life with The Gale.

Name: Tiefling Mage's Study
Content: The location where Mask studied after taking their unauthorized leave of absence from The Gale. A tiefling mage helped Mask refine their newly acquired magical abilities after the lightning strike. This period of study allowed Mask to gain control over their powers before seeking answers in Stormhaven.

Name: Arc's Childhood Home
Content: The location where Arc grew up after her parents were killed for poaching on another changeling's hunting ground. She was sent to live with an aunt, uncle, and cousins whom she disliked. She eventually left them behind to forge her own path.

Name: Sili's Wilderness
Content: The natural environment where Sili developed his connection to nature and his god Azmir. This is where he learned his abilities to communicate with animals and use nature magic, establishing his spiritual bond with the natural world.

Name: Thunder's Respite
Content: A small, storm-rune-inscribed shack nestled in the Blackcloud Reach, featuring a coatroom, common room, guest quarters, and stables. Served as the initial gathering point for the party. Key characters include Eryx, Mira, and various patrons. During the visit, the party met, fought the Iron Tempests, and formed their alliance.

Name: Blackcloud Reach
Content: A region plagued by brutal storms, containing treacherous travel routes and hidden dangers. The party traveled through this area on their way to Stormhaven, encountering Moorbounders, the Olek Tribe, and an underground manor.

Name: The Wandering Haven
Content: A settlement or neighborhood where Fauda lived for a time after leaving her sister Gloriosa. It was here that she befriended the orcish woman Olga Patter. Represents a period of relative peace in Fauda's life.

Name: Underground Manor
Content: A sealed, luxurious home buried beneath a granite pillar, marked with storm runes and a Draconic 'Home' inscription. The party discovered Dragon Cultists, Draconians, and treasure here, including the sigil ring and pendant.

Name: Northwick
Content: A village destroyed by a catastrophic storm after its protective Storm Runes failed. Survivors Tag Brandt, Petra, Tormar, and Ella joined the party briefly on their journey to Stormhaven.

Name: Stormhaven
Content: A massive underground city built beneath mountains, featuring districts like The Crystal Ward, The Emberlight Quarter, and The Pinnacle. The party investigated Lady Seriss's whereabouts, encountered The Order, and allied with Johnny Whisper.

Name: The Cracked Mug
Content: A modest but respectable inn in The Crystal Ward of Stormhaven. Served as the party's primary residence and meeting spot during their time in the city.

Name: The Gilded Lily
Content: A lavish burlesque and performance club in Stormhaven's Crystal Ward. Arc and Dahlia performed here, and the party gathered information from Veska Darrow and other patrons.

Name: The Archivum
Content: A grand library and research facility in The Pinnacle of Stormhaven, controlled by The Order. The party was denied entry initially but later infiltrated it to rescue Lady Seriss.

Name: The Mothlight Inn
Content: A dim tavern in The Shaded Warrens of Stormhaven, frequented by informants and shadowy figures. The party met Johnny Whisper here and received the quest to retrieve The Keledar Codex.

Name: Hargrave Manor
Content: The secluded home of Lord Edwin and Lady Eleanor Hargrave in The Pinnacle. The party infiltrated the manor, discovered a insectoid cult in the tunnels below, and recovered The Keledar Codex.

Name: The Lock & Key
Content: A hidden speakeasy in The Pinnacle, accessed through a bakery. The party learned about Lady Seriss and Zephara Moonveil from the bard Calder Voss here.

Name: Mortimer's Maze
Content: A series of old, dark, and winding corridors located in the lower levels of The Archivum. The party fought Mortimer's forces, rescued Lady Seriss, and escaped through the sewers.

Name: Windrest
Content: A small farming village southeast of the Greenmurk Forest. Its residents were displaced by a giant who declared himself king. The party met refugees from Windrest on the road.

Name: Greenmurk Forest
Content: A forest with trees that absorb and repel storm energy, creating a safe haven from Eryndor's violent weather. The party discovered Cloudfoot Keep here and encountered kobolds and Myconids.

Name: Hollowcoil
Content: A village in the Greenmurk Forest mentioned as a turnoff on the northern forest road. The party passed by it without visiting.

Name: Sewers beneath Ironhowl Forge
Content: The sewer system directly beneath Ironhowl Forge, accessible through a collapsed section of the forge's floor. Contains standard sewer tunnels, grates leading to overflow pipes, and debris from the collapse. Significant as the location of the chaotic battle against the tool-thieving team and their hired muscle. Controlled by city infrastructure, but used by criminals and mercenaries for clandestine movement. Key characters include Korr the Braxat, Zrak'Torr, Voraxik, Shavara, and Kharzid. During the event, the Braxat dropped the safe containing Torva's tools down here, leading to a fight and the party's eventual success in recovering it and capturing the thieves.

Name: Edrin Locke's Private Prison
Content: Hidden prison in Drifthold's 'mid-city' tunnel network between city and underside. Accessed through hidden door in alley leading to plain wooden door marked with embossed shackle. Contains cramped room with single desk, cabinet with collection of gemstones for soul imprisonment. Significant as supernatural prison where party deposited Shavara, Voraxik, and Zrak'Torr. Controlled by Edrin Locke, Chainwright and Ghostspeaker. Key characters include Edrin Locke. During visit: party paid for prisoner 'correction', witnessed soul extraction into gems, learned about Roseheart King's ghostly binding.

Name: Sewers beneath Vale Keep
Content: The sewer network accessible through a secret ash chute and a hidden trap door within Vale Keep. Contains tunnels, sleeping two-headed rat-like abominations with octopus tentacle tails, and a hidden chamber created by the Stone Shape spell. Significant as the secret base of operations for Lucien Shinwell and the location where the party discovered the truth about Lyanna Vale's situation and the plot to reach Solara. Key characters include Lucien Shinwell and Lyanna Vale. During the visit, the party followed Lyanna, met Shinwell, learned his mission, and agreed to help him find 'Rusty' Lou.

Name: The Cogwheel
Content: Tinkerer's shop located above The Guttering Flame tavern in the middle of Drifthold. Contains workshop area with various inventions and dismantled teleportation machine parts. Significant as 'Rusty' Lou's workshop and location where the party negotiated with The Glint Syndicate to secure his assistance. Controlled by The Glint Syndicate during the party's visit. Key characters include 'Rusty' Lou, Mikk 'Scab' Snikk, and various Syndicate enforcers. During visit, party negotiated Lou's release, learned about Aetherglass requirements, and assumed responsibility for his debt.

Name: The Guttering Flame
Content: Tavern located beneath The Cogwheel in Drifthold. Contains main bar area and access to upstairs workshop. Significant as the establishment housing 'Rusty' Lou's workshop. Controlled by tavern owners. Key characters include fox-folk and Skaven guards working for The Glint Syndicate. During visit, party passed through to reach The Cogwheel upstairs.

Name: The Crackling Wand
Content: Magic shop in Drifthold's merchant district, located between a bookstore and a bakery. Contains display counters with magical items and back room for enchanting work. Significant as Leon Gravix's workplace under the alias 'Vix'. Controlled by shop owner. Key characters include Leon Gravix and kindly middle-aged shop owner. During visit, party found Leon working here, warned him about Storm Lord threat, and arranged his evacuation.

Name: Leon's Apartment
Content: Leon Gravix's personal residence in Drifthold. Contains living space filled with dozens of research notebooks detailing storm patterns and historical cataclysms. Significant as location where Leon revealed his research on the 51-year cycle of destruction. Controlled by Leon Gravix. Key characters include Leon Gravix. During visit, Leon gathered his research materials and explained his findings about the imminent cataclysm before evacuating with the party.

Name: The Drowned Bell
Content: Tavern in Drifthold where Korr the Braxat can often be found when not working. Contains bar area where mercenaries gather for work. Significant as one of Korr's regular locations. Controlled by tavern owners. Key characters include Korr. During visit, party found Korr here and warned him about the Storm Lord threat, prompting him to immediately evacuate his family.

Name: Calyx Newdusk's Bottle Realm
Content: A pocket dimension contained within a magical bottle carried by the bounty hunter Calyx Newdusk. Contains two tents housing mercenaries, a large manor, and a central black smoky-glass pyramid covered in runes. The pyramid acts as a one-way prison; Lloyd was thrown inside and cannot escape. Significant as the location where most of the party was trapped after being hit by compressed powder pellets. Controlled by Calyx Newdusk and his mercenary team. Key characters include Calyx Newdusk, several mercenaries, and two four-armed giants. During the ambush, the party was transported here and fought until Calyx offered negotiation.

Name: Duskwatch
Content: A town near the Thundermaw Mountain Range, now under Storm Lord control. Infiltrated and suffered a massacre consistent with Storm Lord tactics. Contains a conversion barn where captured citizens are turned into Storm-Touched soldiers. Many buildings destroyed or smoldering. Key landmark: a large barn with a skylight where lightning strikes down into a conversion apparatus. The town's tavern owner Manfred helped refugees escape through basement tunnels. Current status: Occupied by Storm Lord forces, conversion facility destroyed by party in Session 50.

Name: Duskwatch Conversion Barn
Content: A large barn in Duskwatch used by the Storm Lord to create Storm-Touched soldiers. Contains a glass tank, green and yellow crystals feeding energy into the chamber, and a larger blue crystal similar to lightning batteries. The blue crystal collects lightning from the storm above and distributes it to the other crystals. Dead bodies stacked in some horse stalls, living captives in others. Newly converted soldiers are sent out through a separate door. The party scouted it via Arc's flight and later raided it, destroying the main crystal. Current status: Facility destroyed, crystal exploded in Session 50.

Name: The Prophet
Content: An ancient, enormous statue now weathered into a stone pillar, located near the chasm leading to Duskwatch. Once depicted a great deity but its features have been worn away by storms over centuries. Visible from miles away and serves as a landmark guiding travelers to the bridge across the chasm. Mentioned by Manfred as a navigation point. Current status: Standing landmark, no active significance.

Name: Bridge over the Chasm
Content: A sturdy, magically reinforced bridge spanning a 150‑foot gap, the narrowest point of an immense chasm that drops into dark, swirling oblivion. Debris and powerful gusts rise from below. The bridge is located near The Prophet landmark and provides the only safe crossing to approach Duskwatch. The party crossed it in the late afternoon. Current status: Intact, used by travelers.

Name: The Egg (Council of Keledar Facility)
Content: An enormous egg-shaped structure at least 80 feet tall, made of strange black metal identical to the metal of the hammer-and-crown signet ring. Located near Duskwatch. Glows with bioluminescent green light through seams. Contains multiple interior chambers: a lower chamber with drained energy crystals and metal constructs, a voidling (sentient entity), a glass-and-metal chamber with synthetic amniotic fluid and signs of a giant's birth, an illusion chamber showing the rise of Ormen the World-Breaker and other Storm Lords, an empty armory, a rune chamber, and an exit 80 feet above ground. The facility was used to create and indoctrinate giants as Storm Lords. Controlled by the Council of Keledar. Key entities: the voidling. The party explored it, dispelled mind-controlled guardians, and took a long rest inside. Current status: Abandoned after the giant left.

Name: Thundermaw Mountain Peaks
Content: The high peaks of the Thundermaw Mountain Range, featuring treacherous climbing, narrow ledges, and extreme cold. The party ascended using magical flight and climbing gear. Contains a giant roc nest and later encountered wyvern riders. The area is home to strange dwarves and the entrance to the underground dwarf kingdom. The party camped here and fought rocs. Current status: Traversed by the party.

Name: Thundermaw Dwarf Kingdom
Content: Underground kingdom of dwarves who have been fused with their tools and Aetherglass. Accessible via elevator shaft and winding tunnels that shift and close behind travelers. Ruled by a dwarven king fused with incredible armor. The dwarves worship Khazul, the god of Aetherglass and metamorphosis. Contains the Walk, a sacred trial path through chambers testing submission, sacrifice, and combat against Aetherglass entities. The party undertook the Walk, made sacrifices to Khazul, and received Aetherglass blessings. The kingdom also has a great hall, kitchens, and quarters for visitors. Current status: Party left with a large chunk of Aetherglass, welcome to return.

Name: Harmonia
Content: A seemingly idyllic town under a bright, temperate sky, without storm runes. Ruled by Mayor Alton Fairwright and maintained by automatons (WES, Poppy, Tilly, etc.). The town runs on a loop that resets every few hours, with most citizens having no memory of previous loops. Contains a sawmill, bank, clocktower, jail, school, mess hall, general store, company houses, chapel, tavern, and wellness center. It is actually a constructed experiment of Xyrx the Collector, designed to perfect society. The citizens are either clones or transformed beings. The party experienced multiple loops, discovered the truth, and eventually defeated Xyrx. The town is now bottled and kept at Cloudfoot Keep. Current status: Bottled, under Professor Hubert's supervision.

Name: Harmonia Sawmill
Content: A large sawmill powered by water wheels, with smoke billowing from stacks. Contains a massive cutting saw that malfunctioned, causing an accident that severed Lyanna's arm. The machinery is primarily engineering-based rather than magical, bearing the hammer-and-chisel maker's mark. Statues of Francis Fairwright are in the lobby. The sawmill is central to Harmonia's economy. Controlled by the Fairwright family. Key characters: Foreman, workers. The party investigated it across multiple loops. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Bank
Content: A bank in Harmonia with a large vault containing multiple smaller vaults. Robbed by thieves in one loop, resulting in an explosion that killed the robbers. Contains a vault that held Lyanna's dagger and other valuables. An automaton teller named Poppy operates here. The party investigated the vault and found hidden keys and clues. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Mayor's Office
Content: Office and residence of Mayor Alton Fairwright. Contains paintings of Francis and Manfred Fairwright, a mirror that shows eyes when cleaned, a history book with missing pages, and a hidden chest containing a map of the underground platforms. An automaton guard (Tilly) stands watch. The party questioned Alton here, discovered clues, and found the map. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Clocktower
Content: A massive four-story clocktower, the tallest structure in Harmonia, with six clock faces. Two faces are often under repair. The clock governs the town's schedule and has never had all six faces broken at once. Inside are intricate mechanical systems, gears bearing the hammer-and-chisel maker's mark, and a hidden compartment containing the Tinkerer's Tools (Sparkwright's Trio). Broken clock faces point to the location of the day's disaster. The party investigated and recovered the tools. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Mine Shaft and Elevator
Content: An old mine shaft outside the western wall of Harmonia, containing a freight elevator. The elevator was rigged to plunge and kill users unless unlocked with a special iron key. Leads down to Dave (the mouth entity) and Frank (the bone door), then further into the abyss and Xyrx's lair. The area smells of rotting meat and exhales hot breath. The party discovered the key in the bank painting and used it to descend safely. Current status: Connected to Xyrx's lair, now probably inactive after his defeat.

Name: Harmonia Wellness Center
Content: A medical facility run by Dr. Lysa Fenwick, a cleric of Yondar. Contains examination rooms, supplies, and a jar of Vitalis Root. The doctor cannot regrow limbs but can stabilize patients. The party took Lyanna here after her arm was severed. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Chapel
Content: A multi-faith chapel containing paintings and statues of many gods. Behind the pulpit is a large painted symbol of the Eye of Wu, with the word 'Perfect' hidden within it. The reverend was absent during the party's visit. Fauda discovered the clue here. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: The Even Hand (Harmonia Tavern)
Content: Harmonia's tavern, run by Maribel Tey, a firbolg. Mill workers eat and drink here. The party visited during tours and later used it as a gathering point. Old Tamsin was often arrested for causing disturbances here. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Schoolhouse
Content: A school with a halfling teacher, Mrs. Underbough. Contains a classroom and art supplies. Arc disguised herself as a student and attended class. A heart with initials 'GL+EH' was scratched into the wall. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Company Houses
Content: Residential buildings owned by the sawmill company. They caught fire in one loop, causing deaths. The fire was started by a donkey knocking over a lantern. The party observed the disaster from a distance. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia General Store
Content: A store run by Jonah Pell. Accepts only Company Credit or Harmonia Bucks. Sells mining equipment at discount. The owner mentioned Crownway Provisions as the supplier. The party searched for clues here but found no hidden words. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Jail
Content: A jail run by Sheriff Gorrum Hale, with Deputy Piprik Coilwhistle and automaton Brim. Old Tamsin was frequently held here. Contains a painting of Francis Fairwright with a hidden word 'The'. The party investigated and found clues. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Harmonia Mess Hall
Content: A large dining hall where townsfolk eat. Run by Tansy the cook. Contains a wooden chest refrigerator filled with shredded humanoid remains, and a blood-stained wheelbarrow leading to the river. The party discovered the remains and connected them to the automatons' disposal methods. Current status: In the bottled Harmonia.

Name: Underground Caverns beneath Harmonia
Content: A vast underground network accessible via the mine shaft. Contains Dave (a large mouth entity embedded in the floor) and Frank (a bone door that requires magic words). Further down are a poison room, rickety minecart tracks over an abyss, and a massive amorphous creature (The Hunger) that devours intruders. The party navigated these using Vitalis Root, Drowse Root sedatives, and the correct phrase 'The Perfect Truth of Dreams'. The caverns lead to Xyrx's lair. Current status: Explored by party, creature sedated.

Name: Xyrx's Lair
Content: The subterranean lair of Xyrx the Collector, accessed via the underground caverns. Contains multiple chambers: a teleportation platform with rune circles, a cloning chamber, a Transfusion Room (for creating draconic hybrids), a Dream-Creation Chamber (with orb view of Harmonia), a Vision Chamber (surveillance of Eryndor), Zrak'tor's quarters, and Xyrx's personal chambers. The lair is filled with magical items, bottled prisoners, and Beholder-created minions. The party fought Xyrx and his forces here, defeated him, and rescued many prisoners. Current status: Abandoned after Xyrx's death, but teleportation circles remain.

Name: Teleportation Platform (Xyrx's Lair)
Content: A platform within Xyrx's lair containing a rune circle network. One rune connects to Harmonia, one to Solara's Central Teleportation Hub, one directly to House Octo's keep in Solara, and eight others to various vaults across Eryndor. The party used it to return to Tempest's Watch. The rune sequences were copied for future use. Current status: Accessible if the party returns to the lair.

Name: Gloomwatch Sanctum
Content: A subterranean city located at the outer edge of the Blackstorm Peaks within the Upper Underdark. Ruled primarily by Upper Drow (who differ from Deep Drow). A major mining city populated by Upper Drow, Duergar, and Dwarves, though many other races can also be found. The party is traveling there as their next destination after leaving Cloudfoot Keep. Current status: Not yet visited, but the party is en route.
